title: Recommend native overlayfs instead of fuse-overlayfs?
body:
Hi, according to https://www.redhat.com/sysadmin/podman-rootless-overlay is it still recommended to use `mount_program = "/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs"` when Kernel 5.13 has shipped native support?
Anything else to consider except clearing the container storage and comment `# mount_program = "/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs"`?

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/34d516840df73787d2359037e4a2ab51849e59eb/contrib/podmanimage/stable/Containerfile#L38-L43

comments.body:
I don't think that blog says we still recommend `fuse-overlayfs` when native Overlay is available; we prefer native overlay unless you have a good reason to continue to use fuse-overlay (it does some things we don't have kernel support for, at least last I checked; UID mapping would be the most notable).

Commenting the `mount_program` line will indeed be sufficient to swap the driver.
